title: BOM Decomposition — NAICS 221114 Solar Electric Power Generation

sections.title: 0 — Scope and routing
sections.content:
| Decision | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| End-product BOM | **Skipped** | Electricity = L0 commodity; per component doc NAICS routing, commodity output types do not have compositional BOMs. |
| Capital-asset BOM | **Built** (this document) | The Solar PV plant is the L5 capital asset the industry operates; Mein Solar's BESS sits inside it as an L4 subsystem. |
| Company position | **L4 BESS subsystem** | Mein Solar EPCs the whole L4 Battery Energy Storage block (all its L3→L0 branches). |

---

sections.title: B.1 — L4 Subsystems of the Solar+BESS Plant
sections.content:
```
L5  Utility-Scale Solar PV Power Plant with co-located BESS
|
+-- L4.1  PV Generation Subsystem         (primary functional)
+-- L4.2  BESS Subsystem                   (primary functional)  ← [Mein Solar] position
+-- L4.3  Power Conversion Subsystem (PCS) (primary functional)
+-- L4.4  MV Grid Coupling Subsystem       (interface/control)
+-- L4.5  EMS & SCADA Subsystem            (interface/control)
+-- L4.6  Civil & Balance-of-Plant         (structural/auxiliary)
```

### L5 → L4 alternatives (plant architecture)

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Solar-only (no storage) | 55% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | WoodMac H1'25 utility solar mix |
| DC-coupled PV+BESS | 15% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | WoodMac / IEA hybrid tracker |
| AC-coupled PV+BESS (co-located) | 25% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | Solar+Storage hybrid 2024–26 |
| PV+BESS retrofit (BESS added to existing PV) | 5% | ↑ Emerging | MEDIUM | Analyst estimate |

Shares sum: 100%.

---

sections.title: B.2 — L4.1 PV Generation Subsystem (full L4→L0)
sections.content:
### L4.1 → L3

```
L4.1 PV Generation
  L3.1.1 PV Module Array
  L3.1.2 Module Mounting / Tracking Structure
  L3.1.3 DC Combining & Protection
  L3.1.4 DC Cabling
```

| L3 alternative (architecture) | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| All four L3 blocks present (standard utility config) | 100% | → Stable | HIGH | `single-source: true` — every utility PV plant contains all four |

### L3.1.1 PV Module Array → L2 PV Strings → L1 PV Module → L0 Semiconductor / Cover

**L3→L2 (module technology):**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| TOPCon n-type | 49% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | InfoLink 2025 shipment data |
| PERC p-type | 40% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | InfoLink 2025 |
| HJT n-type | 7% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | Industry analyst 2025 |
| Back-contact / tandem / other n-type | 4% | ↑ Emerging | LOW | Industry analyst 2025 |

**L2→L1 (cell + encapsulation):** `single-source: true` — every module has cell + glass + EVA + backsheet + frame.

**L1→L0 (raw material of the cell):**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Monocrystalline silicon wafer | 98% | → Stable | HIGH | ITRPV 2025 |
| Thin-film (CdTe, CIGS) | 2% | → Stable | MEDIUM | First Solar share in utility segment |

**L1→L0 (cover glass):** Low-iron tempered solar glass — `single-source: true`, 100%, HIGH, IEC 61215.

**L1→L0 (frame):** Anodized aluminum alloy — `single-source: true`, 100%, HIGH, industry standard.

### L3.1.2 Mounting Structure → L2 Racking → L1 Structural Member → L0 Steel

**L3→L2 (architecture):**

| Alternative | Share (utility-scale 2025) | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Single-axis tracker | 80% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | GMI / Mordor tracker reports |
| Fixed-tilt rack | 18% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | GMI 2025 |
| Dual-axis tracker | 1% | → Stable | HIGH | Mordor 2025 |
| Floating PV structure | 1% | ↑ Emerging | MEDIUM | NREL FPV 2024 |

**L2→L1:** Torque tube + purlin + pile — `single-source: true`, 100%, HIGH.

**L1→L0:**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Hot-dip galvanized carbon steel | 88% | → Stable | HIGH | ASTM A123 industry standard |
| Aluminum extrusion | 10% | → Stable | MEDIUM | Rooftop-derived utility niche |
| Zinc-magnesium-aluminum (ZMA) coated steel | 2% | ↑ Emerging | MEDIUM | ArcelorMittal Magnelis share |

### L3.1.3 DC Combining → L2 Combiner Box → L1 PCB/Fuse Assembly → L0 Copper / FR4

**L3→L2:**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| String combiner box (1500 V DC) | 90% | → Stable | HIGH | Utility standard |
| Smart combiner with MLPE | 10% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | Analyst estimate |

**L2→L1:** Fuses + DC switch + busbar — `single-source: true`, 100%, HIGH.
**L1→L0:** Electrolytic copper (busbar/wire) + FR-4 (PCB) + polycarbonate (enclosure) — `single-source: true`, HIGH.

### L3.1.4 DC Cabling → L2 Cable Run → L1 Solar Cable → L0 Copper + XLPE

**L3→L2:** PV-rated DC cable (H1Z2Z2-K, 1500 V) — `single-source: true`, 100%, HIGH, EN 50618.
**L2→L1:** Conductor + XLPE insulation + EPDM sheath — `single-source: true`, HIGH.
**L1→L0:**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Electrolytic copper conductor | 95% | → Stable | HIGH | IEC 60228 |
| Aluminum conductor (AL-PV for long runs) | 5% | ↑ Emerging | LOW | Analyst estimate |

---

sections.title: B.2 — L4.2 BESS Subsystem ← [Mein Solar] position (full L4→L0)
sections.content:
### L4.2 → L3

```
L4.2 BESS Subsystem                                          ← [Mein Solar] position (full scope)
  L3.2.1 Battery String / Rack
  L3.2.2 Battery Management System (BMS / BAMS / BCMS)
  L3.2.3 Thermal Management (HVAC + liquid cooling)
  L3.2.4 Fire Protection & Deflagration Venting
  L3.2.5 Container Envelope
```

All five L3 blocks are `single-source: true` at architecture level — every containerized BESS has all five.

### L3.2.1 Battery Rack → L2 Battery Module → L1 Battery Cell → L0 Active Material

**L3→L2 (chemistry):**

| Alternative | Share (stationary 2025) | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| LFP (LiFePO₄) prismatic | 85% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | WoodMac 2025 LFP-dominance |
| NMC pouch/prismatic | 12% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | WoodMac 2025 |
| Sodium-ion | 2% | ↑ Emerging | MEDIUM | CATL / HiNa 2025 pilots |
| LTO / other | 1% | → Stable | MEDIUM | Toshiba SCiB niche |

**L2→L1 (cell format):**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 314 Ah prismatic cell | 55% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | CATL/EVE/CALB standard 2025 |
| 280 Ah prismatic cell | 35% | ↓ Declining | MEDIUM | Legacy BESS standard 2022–24 |
| 500+ Ah prismatic cell | 8% | ↑ Emerging | MEDIUM | EVE Mr.Big / CATL 587 Ah |
| Pouch / cylindrical cell | 2% | → Stable | LOW | Analyst estimate |

**L1→L0 (cathode active material):**

| Alternative | Share (LFP branch) | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| LiFePO₄ (virgin synthesis) | 97% | → Stable | HIGH | IEA Critical Minerals 2025 |
| LiFePO₄ from recycled Fe/Li | 3% | ↑ Emerging | LOW | EU Battery Reg. 2025 target |

**L1→L0 (anode):** Synthetic/natural graphite — `single-source: true`, 100%, HIGH, IEA 2025 (silicon-doped <2% in stationary).

**L1→L0 (electrolyte):** LiPF₆ in carbonate solvent blend — `single-source: true`, 100%, HIGH, industry standard.

**L1→L0 (separator):** PE/PP microporous film (ceramic-coated) — `single-source: true`, 100%, HIGH.

**L1→L0 (current collectors):** Al foil (cathode) + Cu foil (anode) — `single-source: true`, 100%, HIGH.

### L3.2.2 Battery Management System → L2 Control Boards → L1 PCB Assembly → L0 Cu/Si/FR4

**L3→L2 (BMS architecture):**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 3-level (BMU/BCMS/BAMS hierarchical) | 80% | → Stable | HIGH | Industry standard |
| 2-level (flat slave-master) | 20% | ↓ Declining | MEDIUM | Legacy small-BESS arch |

**L2→L1:** PCB with MCU + ADC + balancing FETs + isoSPI — `single-source: true`, 100%, HIGH.
**L1→L0:** Copper + FR-4 substrate + silicon (IC), `single-source: true`, HIGH.

### L3.2.3 Thermal Management → L2 Cooling Loop → L1 Heat Exchanger/Pump → L0 Al/Glycol

**L3→L2 (thermal architecture):**

| Alternative | Share (utility BESS 2025) | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Liquid cooling (glycol-water) | 85% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | Sungrow PowerTitan, CATL EnerC |
| Air cooling (forced HVAC) | 13% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | Legacy 2019–22 arch |
| Immersion cooling | 2% | ↑ Emerging | LOW | Analyst (PowerMag 2025) |

**L2→L1:** Pump + brazed-plate heat exchanger + manifolds — `single-source: true`, 100%, HIGH.
**L1→L0:** Aluminum (heat exchanger), ethylene-glycol/water 50/50 (coolant), copper (piping) — `single-source: true`, HIGH.

### L3.2.4 Fire Protection → L2 Suppression Skid → L1 Tank/Nozzle → L0 Agent

**L3→L2 (suppression architecture):**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Clean-agent gas (NOVEC 1230 / FM-200) | 55% | → Stable | MEDIUM | Control Fire Systems 2025 review |
| Aerosol (potassium salt, Stat-X / FirePro) | 25% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | Industry best-practice shift |
| Water mist (high-pressure) | 15% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | UL 9540A trend |
| Dry chemical / CO₂ | 5% | ↓ Declining | MEDIUM | Legacy |

**L2→L1:** Agent cylinder + distribution pipework + detectors — `single-source: true`, 100%, HIGH.
**L1→L0 (agent):**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NOVEC 1230 (FK-5-1-12) fluorinated ketone | 55% | → Stable | HIGH | 3M / Chemours product data |
| Potassium-salt aerosol | 25% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | Fireaway / FirePro |
| Deionized water + additives | 15% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | Marioff HI-FOG |
| Heptafluoropropane (FM-200) | 5% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | Chemours |

### L3.2.5 Container Envelope → L2 ISO Container → L1 Panel/Frame → L0 Steel

**L3→L2 (form factor):**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 20 ft ISO HC (1–3 MWh) | 65% | → Stable | HIGH | Sungrow/CATL catalog |
| 40 ft ISO HC (3–6 MWh) | 25% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | Tesla Megapack 2 trend |
| Bespoke skid (non-ISO) | 10% | ↓ Declining | MEDIUM | Legacy integrators |

**L2→L1:** Corten/steel panels + door frame + seal — `single-source: true`, 100%, HIGH, ISO 1496.
**L1→L0:** Corten (weathering) steel + intumescent coating + aluminum insulation — `single-source: true`, HIGH.

---

sections.title: B.2 — L4.3 PCS Subsystem (full L4→L0)
sections.content:
### L4.3 → L3

```
L4.3 Power Conversion Subsystem
  L3.3.1 Bidirectional Inverter (PCS unit)
  L3.3.2 DC Link & Capacitor Bank
  L3.3.3 PCS Controller
  L3.3.4 PCS Cooling
```

All four L3 blocks `single-source: true` at architecture level.

### L3.3.1 Bidirectional Inverter → L2 Power Stack → L1 Semiconductor Module → L0 Si/SiC

**L3→L2 (inverter architecture):**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Central PCS (1.5–5 MW units, 1500 V DC) | 75% | → Stable | HIGH | Utility-scale norm |
| String PCS (100–300 kW units) | 20% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | Huawei/Sungrow string trend |
| Modular/stackable 2-level (>2 MW blocks) | 5% | ↑ Emerging | MEDIUM | Analyst estimate |

**L2→L1 (OEM / supplier):**

| Alternative | Share (BESS PCS + PV inverter combined) | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Sungrow | 28% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | WoodMac H1'25 inverter ranking |
| Huawei | 27% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | WoodMac H1'25 |
| SMA Solar Technology | 8% | → Stable | HIGH | WoodMac H1'25 (#3 globally) |
| Power Electronics (ES) | 6% | → Stable | MEDIUM | Utility PCS share |
| Ingeteam | 4% | → Stable | MEDIUM | European utility share |
| TBEA / Ginlong / Fronius / other | 27% | → Stable | MEDIUM | WoodMac residual |

**L1→L0 (switching device):**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Silicon IGBT | 85% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | Standard utility PCS |
| SiC MOSFET | 12% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | Sungrow/Huawei SiC adoption |
| GaN (low-power aux) | 3% | ↑ Emerging | LOW | Analyst estimate |

### L3.3.2 DC Link → L2 Busbar + Capacitor Bank → L1 Capacitor → L0 Al electrolyte / film

**L3→L2:** 1500 V DC link with film/electrolytic caps — `single-source: true`, 100%, HIGH.
**L2→L1:**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Polypropylene film capacitor | 70% | → Stable | HIGH | Utility PCS standard |
| Aluminum electrolytic | 30% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | Legacy |

**L1→L0:** Polypropylene film + aluminum foil (film cap) OR aluminum + electrolyte (electrolytic) — `single-source: true`, HIGH.

### L3.3.3 PCS Controller → L2 Controller Card → L1 PCB → L0 Cu/Si/FR4

Mirror L3.2.2 — `single-source: true` at L2, L1; L0 is Cu + FR-4 + Si.

### L3.3.4 PCS Cooling → L2 Cooling Loop → L1 HX/Pump → L0 Al/Glycol

**L3→L2:**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Liquid cooling (glycol) | 75% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | Utility PCS standard |
| Forced air cooling | 25% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | Legacy |

**L2→L1:** Pump + HX + fan — `single-source: true`, 100%, HIGH.
**L1→L0:** Aluminum + glycol/water + copper piping — `single-source: true`, HIGH.

---

sections.title: B.2 — L4.4 MV Grid Coupling Subsystem (full L4→L0)
sections.content:
### L4.4 → L3

```
L4.4 MV Grid Coupling
  L3.4.1 Step-up Transformer (LV→MV)
  L3.4.2 MV Switchgear (RMU)
  L3.4.3 Protection & Metering
  L3.4.4 NA-Protection & Grid Interface Cabinet
```

All four L3 blocks `single-source: true` at architecture level (required by VDE-AR-N 4110).

### L3.4.1 Step-up Transformer → L2 Core+Winding → L1 Lamination/Wire → L0 GOES/Cu

**L3→L2 (technology):**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Oil-immersed distribution transformer | 70% | → Stable | HIGH | European utility norm |
| Cast-resin dry-type | 28% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | Indoor/container preference |
| Ester-filled (biodegradable) | 2% | ↑ Emerging | MEDIUM | ESG-driven adoption |

**L2→L1:** Core + HV winding + LV winding + tank + bushings — `single-source: true`, 100%, HIGH.
**L1→L0 (core):**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Grain-oriented electrical steel (GOES) | 97% | → Stable | HIGH | IEC 60404 |
| Amorphous metal | 3% | ↑ Emerging | MEDIUM | Hitachi Metglas |

**L1→L0 (winding):** Electrolytic copper (rarely aluminum) — `single-source: true`, 100%, HIGH.

### L3.4.2 MV Switchgear → L2 RMU → L1 Vacuum Circuit Breaker → L0 Cu/Steel

**L3→L2 (insulation):**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| SF₆-insulated GIS | 55% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | Legacy European norm |
| SF₆-free (Clean Air / dry air / g³) | 35% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | Siemens 8DAB-blue, Schneider SM AirSeT |
| Solid-insulated (epoxy / silicone) | 10% | → Stable | MEDIUM | ABB HySec |

**L2→L1 (breaker):** Vacuum circuit breaker — `single-source: true`, 100%, HIGH (IEC 62271-100 standard).

**L1→L0 (VCB contacts + housing):** Copper-chrome contacts + stainless-steel bellows + ceramic (Al₂O₃) envelope — `single-source: true`, HIGH.

**L2→L1 (OEM / vendor):**

| Alternative | Share (Europe MV 2025) | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Siemens | 22% | → Stable | HIGH | MarketsandMarkets Europe MV 2025 |
| ABB | 21% | → Stable | HIGH | MarketsandMarkets 2025 |
| Schneider Electric | 20% | → Stable | HIGH | MarketsandMarkets 2025 |
| Eaton | 8% | → Stable | MEDIUM | MarketsandMarkets 2025 |
| Hitachi Energy | 6% | → Stable | MEDIUM | MarketsandMarkets 2025 |
| Other (Ormazabal, Lucy, Driescher, Efacec) | 23% | → Stable | MEDIUM | Residual |

### L3.4.3 Protection & Metering → L2 Relay + CT/PT → L1 PCB/Core → L0 Si/Cu/SiSteel

**L3→L2:** Numerical protection relay + metering-class CT/VT — `single-source: true`, 100%, HIGH.
**L2→L1:** Relay PCB + CT core + VT core — `single-source: true`, 100%, HIGH.
**L1→L0:**

| Alternative (CT/VT core) | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Non-oriented silicon steel | 92% | → Stable | HIGH | IEC 60404 |
| Nanocrystalline (VITROPERM) | 8% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | Vacuumschmelze |

### L3.4.4 NA-Protection Cabinet → L2 Relay Cabinet → L1 PCB → L0 Cu/FR4/Si

**L3→L2:** Certified NA-Schutz relay (VDE-AR-N 4110 decoupling) — `single-source: true`, 100%, HIGH.
**L2→L1 + L1→L0:** Relay + PCB → Cu + FR-4 + Si, `single-source: true`, HIGH.

---

sections.title: B.2 — L4.5 EMS & SCADA Subsystem (full L4→L0)
sections.content:
### L4.5 → L3

```
L4.5 EMS & SCADA
  L3.5.1 EMS Controller (industrial PC / PLC)
  L3.5.2 SCADA Server & HMI
  L3.5.3 Network & Gateway Hardware
  L3.5.4 Measurement / Telemetry Sensors
```

All four L3 blocks `single-source: true` at architecture level.

Note: EMS *software* is a digital asset — it sits **above** the BOM (L6 Service layer). Only the physical compute/networking hardware is decomposed here.

### L3.5.1 EMS Controller → L2 Industrial PC → L1 Motherboard → L0 Si/FR4

**L3→L2 (platform):**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Industrial PC (x86) | 55% | → Stable | HIGH | Utility-scale EMS norm |
| PLC (Siemens S7-1500, B&R) | 30% | → Stable | HIGH | Legacy industrial norm |
| Edge gateway (ARM-based) | 15% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | Fluence Nispera, Power Factors |

**L2→L1:** Motherboard + CPU + RAM + storage — `single-source: true`, 100%, HIGH.
**L1→L0:** Silicon (CPU/chips) + FR-4 + copper — `single-source: true`, HIGH.

### L3.5.2 SCADA Server → L2 Rack Server → L1 Server Board → L0 Si/FR4

**L3→L2 (deployment):**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| On-premise rack server | 60% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | Utility-mandated (IEC 62443) |
| Hybrid on-prem + cloud | 35% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | Power Factors / AlsoEnergy |
| Full cloud SCADA | 5% | ↑ Emerging | LOW | Aggregator-led deployments |

**L2→L1:** Server motherboard + CPU + RAM + NIC — `single-source: true`, 100%, HIGH.
**L1→L0:** Silicon + FR-4 + copper + aluminum chassis — `single-source: true`, HIGH.

### L3.5.3 Network Gateway → L2 Switch + Gateway → L1 PCB → L0 Si/Cu/FR4

**L3→L2 (protocol stack):**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| IEC 61850 over Ethernet (utility) | 50% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | DSO interoperability requirement |
| Modbus-TCP / DNP3 | 40% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | Legacy |
| OPC-UA | 10% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | IEC 61968 / industrial IoT trend |

**L2→L1:** Managed Ethernet switch + fiber gateway — `single-source: true`, 100%, HIGH.
**L1→L0:** Silicon + FR-4 + copper — `single-source: true`, HIGH.

### L3.5.4 Sensors → L2 Sensor Assembly → L1 Transducer → L0 Si/Cu

**L3→L2:**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Pyranometer + temp + wind (PV-side) | 100% | → Stable | HIGH | `single-source: true` — IEC 61724-1 |

**L2→L1 + L1→L0:** Thermopile/Si-photodiode + Cu/FR-4 → Silicon + glass + copper — `single-source: true`, HIGH.

---

sections.title: B.2 — L4.6 Civil & Balance-of-Plant (full L4→L0)
sections.content:
### L4.6 → L3

```
L4.6 Civil / BoP
  L3.6.1 Foundations (BESS pad + inverter pad + transformer pad)
  L3.6.2 Access Roads & Site Grading
  L3.6.3 Fencing, Perimeter & Security
  L3.6.4 Earthing & Lightning Protection
  L3.6.5 Cable Trenching & Conduits
```

All five L3 blocks `single-source: true` at architecture level.

### L3.6.1 Foundations → L2 Concrete Pad → L1 Concrete Block → L0 Cement/Aggregate/Steel

**L3→L2:**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Reinforced concrete pad (cast in situ) | 70% | → Stable | HIGH | Utility PV+BESS standard |
| Precast concrete pad | 20% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | Modular-deployment trend |
| Screw piles / micropiles (driven) | 10% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | Tracker foundation norm |

**L2→L1:** Concrete + rebar cage + anchor bolts — `single-source: true`, 100%, HIGH.
**L1→L0:** Portland cement + aggregate (sand/gravel) + water + reinforcing steel — `single-source: true`, HIGH.

### L3.6.2 Access Roads → L2 Gravel Subbase → L1 Aggregate Layer → L0 Crushed Stone

**L3→L2:** Compacted gravel access road — `single-source: true`, 100%, HIGH, DIN 18300.
**L2→L1→L0:** Crushed stone + geotextile — `single-source: true`, HIGH.

### L3.6.3 Fencing → L2 Fence Panel → L1 Wire Mesh → L0 Galvanized Steel

**L3→L2:**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Welded mesh panel (2 m, anti-climb) | 90% | → Stable | HIGH | VDE fire-protection norm |
| Chain-link fabric | 10% | ↓ Declining | MEDIUM | Legacy |

**L2→L1→L0:** Wire mesh + posts → galvanized carbon steel — `single-source: true`, HIGH.

### L3.6.4 Earthing → L2 Ground Grid → L1 Ground Rod → L0 Copper/Copper-clad Steel

**L3→L2:**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Buried copper grid + rods | 75% | → Stable | HIGH | IEC 62305 |
| Copper-clad steel | 25% | → Stable | HIGH | Cost-optimized variant |

**L2→L1→L0:** Cu conductor + bentonite backfill — `single-source: true`, HIGH.

### L3.6.5 Cable Trenching → L2 Trench + Conduit → L1 Conduit → L0 HDPE

**L3→L2:** Open-cut trench + HDPE conduit + warning tape — `single-source: true`, 100%, HIGH.
**L2→L1→L0:** HDPE conduit + sand bedding — `single-source: true`, HIGH.

---

sections.title: B.3 — Alternatives summary at company position
sections.content:
**Mein Solar delivers the entire L4.2 BESS subsystem.** All L3 → L0 branches inside L4.2 are decomposed above. Key alternatives at the company position (the *kind* of BESS subsystem Mein Solar offers) versus customer-chosen substitutes:

| Alternative at L4 position | Share (utility PV+BESS co-location 2025) | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| AC-coupled LFP BESS subsystem (Mein Solar's target) | 70% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | WoodMac hybrid PV+BESS 2025 |
| DC-coupled LFP BESS subsystem | 25% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | WoodMac 2025 |
| NMC BESS subsystem | 3% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | WoodMac chemistry mix |
| Sodium-ion BESS subsystem (pilot) | 1% | ↑ Emerging | MEDIUM | CATL/HiNa 2025 |
| Flow-battery (vanadium/iron-salt) | 1% | ↑ Emerging | MEDIUM | Invinity / ESS Inc pilots |

---

sections.title: B.4 — Component category mapping (selected — cross-subsystem view)
sections.content:
Only categories where a single component appears in multiple L4 subsystems.

### Inverter / PCS IC modules

| Position | Function | Safety-critical | Alternatives at position |
|---|---|---|---|
| L4.3 > L3.3.1 > L2 Power stack > L1 IGBT/SiC | Main DC↔AC conversion (BESS + PV) | YES | Si-IGBT 85% / SiC-MOSFET 12% / GaN 3% |
| L4.1 > L3.1.3 > L2 Smart combiner > L1 Optimizer IGBT | PV MPPT per-string | NO | Same as above, smaller die |

### Liquid cooling loop

| Position | Function | Safety-critical | Alternatives at position |
|---|---|---|---|
| L4.2 > L3.2.3 > L2 Battery cooling loop | Cell thermal control (±2 K) | YES | Glycol/water 85% / immersion 2% / air 13% |
| L4.3 > L3.3.4 > L2 PCS cooling loop | IGBT junction cooling | YES | Glycol/water 75% / air 25% |

### PCB / FR-4 substrate

| Position | Function | Safety-critical | Alternatives |
|---|---|---|---|
| L4.2 > L3.2.2 > BMS PCB | Cell balancing & monitoring | YES | Standard FR-4 (industry norm) |
| L4.3 > L3.3.3 > PCS controller | Inverter gate drive | YES | High-Tg FR-4 |
| L4.4 > L3.4.3 > Protection relay | IED protection | YES | High-Tg FR-4 |
| L4.5 > L3.5.1 > EMS controller | Dispatch logic | NO | Standard FR-4 |

### Copper

| Position | Function | Alternatives |
|---|---|---|
| L4.1 DC cable | PV DC transport | Cu 95% / Al 5% |
| L4.2 Cell current collector | Anode conduction | Cu foil 100% |
| L4.3 DC busbar | PCS DC link | Cu 98% / Al 2% |
| L4.4 Transformer winding | MV transformation | Cu 98% / Al 2% |
| L4.6 Earthing | Ground-fault path | Cu 75% / Cu-clad steel 25% |

---

sections.title: B.5 — Company position marking
sections.content:
```
L5  Utility-Scale Solar PV Power Plant with co-located BESS
|
+-- L4.1  PV Generation Subsystem
+-- L4.2  BESS Subsystem                     ← [Mein Solar GmbH] position (full scope, L4 all the way down)
+-- L4.3  Power Conversion Subsystem
+-- L4.4  MV Grid Coupling Subsystem
+-- L4.5  EMS & SCADA Subsystem
+-- L4.6  Civil & Balance-of-Plant
```

**Position classification:** Mein Solar is **not** a component supplier. Mein Solar is an **L4-subsystem integrator (EPC)** — it acquires L2/L1 components (cells, containers, PCS, MV equipment) from upstream suppliers and delivers the complete L4.2 BESS subsystem commissioned and grid-certified into the L5 plant.

**Two BOMs apply (per component doc B.5 rule):**
1. **Upward context BOM** — this document (L5 plant with L4.2 marked). Drives adjacency analysis below.
2. **Downward product BOM** — L4.2 BESS decomposed L3 → L0 (done in §B.2 above). Drives supply-chain and material strategy.

---

sections.title: B.6 — Strategic BOM adjacency
sections.content:
### Horizontal BOM adjacency (other L4 subsystems at the same plant)

| Adjacent L4 component | Capability gap | Market size (EU utility PV+BESS) | Strategic fit |
|---|---|---|---|
| L4.1 PV Generation Subsystem | **LOW** | ~€12 B/yr (DE utility PV installs 2025) | **STRONG** — Mein Solar incumbent business |
| L4.3 PCS Subsystem | MEDIUM | ~€1.5 B/yr (DE PCS share of PV+BESS) | WEAK — Sungrow/Huawei/SMA locked |
| L4.4 MV Grid Coupling Subsystem | MEDIUM | ~€0.8 B/yr (DE utility MV-coupling BoP) | MEDIUM — already in scope of EPC delivery |
| L4.5 EMS & SCADA Subsystem | HIGH (software product) | ~€0.3 B/yr (DE utility EMS market) | WEAK — Fluence/Power Factors/Next Kraftwerke locked |
| L4.6 Civil / BoP Subsystem | LOW | ~€1.2 B/yr (DE utility PV+BESS civil) | STRONG — already in scope of EPC delivery |

**Conclusion:** Mein Solar already horizontally covers L4.1 (PV), L4.4 (MV coupling), and L4.6 (civil) as part of the EPC contract. The non-covered L4s (L4.3 PCS, L4.5 EMS) are equipment-vendor markets where L2 OEMs hold the value — not natural EPC extensions.

### Vertical BOM move (up) — from L4 subsystem to L5 plant-level EPC

| Factor | Evaluation |
|---|---|
| Move | L4 BESS subsystem EPC → L5 full Solar+BESS plant EPC (already Mein Solar's PV business extended with BESS) |
| Additional capabilities required | None — Mein Solar already does L5 PV plant EPC; adding L4.2 extends scope, not level |
| Capital investment | Low — commercial and engineering, not fixed-asset |
| Customer leverage | **HIGH** — existing PV customers are the highest-probability BESS retrofit customers |
| Competitive response risk | Tier-1 integrators (Fluence, Tesla, Sungrow) may counter with PV-retrofit bundles |
| Time to market | 6–12 months (already underway: 4.5 MW / 9 MWh reference installed) |

**Conclusion:** The L4 → L5 move is the Mein Solar strategic thesis and is already in execution.

### Vertical BOM move (down) / cross-system — BESS subsystem in non-PV plants

| Target system (non-PV) | BOM position | Shared technology | Adaptation needed |
|---|---|---|---|
| Wind park (NAICS 221115) | L4 BESS in HV/MV wind-park asset | 100% — same L4 decomposition | Grid code VDE-AR-N 4120 for HV sites |
| Standalone BESS IPP (NAICS 221118) | L5 plant *is* the BESS | 100% — same L4 decomposition (no PV, no civil-sharing) | Bigger share of revenue from BESS; commercial terms |
| DSO Netzbooster (NAICS 221122) | L4 BESS in substation asset | 100% — same L4 | IEC 61850 interoperability + utility procurement rigor |
| Data center UPS+BESS (NAICS 518210) | L4 BESS behind LV UPS | 90% — LV coupling instead of MV | 99.999% availability spec, UL 9540A fire scrutiny |
| HPC charging hub (NAICS 457120) | L4 BESS buffering DC-fast chargers | 85% — smaller scale, LV+MV mixed | HPC dispenser interface |
| Electrolyzer site (NAICS 325120) | L4 BESS for ramp firming | 80% — EMS co-optimized with electrolyzer | Hydrogen-plant control logic |

**Conclusion:** The L4.2 BESS subsystem is **portable across every market in §05 market discovery** with minimal adaptation — the cell/container/PCS/MV-coupling/fire stack is identical; only the EMS dispatch logic and grid-code scope change by customer.

---

sections.title: QA Checklist
sections.content:
| Check | Status |
|---|---|
| Every output type has its own BOM tree | ✅ (single capital asset: Solar+BESS plant) |
| Every L4 subsystem decomposes all the way to L0 | ✅ (L4.1–L4.6 all traced to L0) |
| Every level transition has an alternatives table | ✅ (including `single-source: true` rows) |
| Alternative shares sum to 100% (±1%) | ✅ |
| Packaging/containment subsystem included | ✅ (L3.2.5 Container Envelope) |
| Control/interface subsystem included | ✅ (L4.5 EMS & SCADA) |
| Company position marked | ✅ (L4.2 ← [Mein Solar] position) |
| Horizontal + vertical adjacency done (B.6) | ✅ |
| BOM vs VN boundary check | ✅ — all items end up IN the plant; manufacturing equipment (cell production lines, container welding robots) are in VN not BOM |

---

rows.Rationale: Electricity = L0 commodity; per component doc NAICS routing, commodity output types do not have compositional BOMs.
The Solar PV plant is the L5 capital asset the industry operates; Mein Solar's BESS sits inside it as an L4 subsystem.
Mein Solar EPCs the whole L4 Battery Energy Storage block (all its L3→L0 branches).
